message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "standard sword damage 500 G yields 400 G payout"
Current error: claim throws "not implemented"
Passing tests so far: 19

Working directory: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-11-17_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking
Run tests with: pnpm test

Implement claim minimally. The test:
  claim({
    policy: { items: [{type:"sword", material:"steel", enchantment:3, cursed:false}], remainingCap: 2000 },
    incident: { cause: "fire", damages: [{itemType:"sword", amount:500}] }
  })
expects { payout: 400, remainingCap: 1600 }

Per spec: standard reimbursement = full damage minus 100 G deductible. payout = 500 - 100 = 400.
remainingCap = 2000 - 400 = 1600.

Implement minimally — single damage to a single item, no special clauses (no enchantment≥8, no dragon material). Hardcoding constants like DEDUCTIBLE=100 is good. The claim signature accepts `{ policy, incident }` and returns `{ payout, remainingCap }`.

Do NOT refactor.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll implement the minimal code to make the failing test pass.

content.text:
All 20 tests pass (19 previously passing + 1 newly passing).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added minimal `claim` function that computes payout as `damage - 100` (deductible) and `remainingCap` as policy's remaining cap minus payout.
- Result: All 20 tests pass (was 19, now 20).
- Approach: Minimal - directly accesses the first damage amount, hardcodes the 100 G deductible, no loops, no special clauses (no enchantment/dragon handling), no validation. Just enough to satisfy the single active test.

File: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-17_01-11-17_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts`

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
